frame_analyses.description: Setup view showing training pad with circular markings and no instruments visible
Both instruments visible, title overlay shows 'Laparoscopic intracorporeal slip knot', suture visible between instruments
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task has begun, instruments positioned for knot tying

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver holds suture, left instrument assists in loop formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial loop construction for slip knot technique

frame_analyses.description: Suture loop being manipulated, both instruments working to form the slip knot configuration
C-loop label visible, red dot marking on suture, instruments continuing loop formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: C-loop formation is a key component of slip knot technique

frame_analyses.description: Suture loop more defined, instruments maintaining good positioning
Loop formation progressing with both instruments coordinating movement
Complex loop structure visible, suture being threaded through loop
Loop tightening phase, instruments working to secure the slip knot
Slip knot formation nearly complete, suture ends being managed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good control of suture ends during tightening

frame_analyses.description: First knot complete, suture ends positioned for additional throws
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean completion of initial slip knot

frame_analyses.description: Beginning second throw, 'Unlocking the knot' text overlay visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Transition to securing throws, unlocking refers to preventing slip

frame_analyses.description: 'Sliding the knot' text overlay, instruments positioning for knot advancement
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot sliding technique to position knot properly

frame_analyses.description: 'Locking the knot' text with red highlight, instruments securing the knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Locking phase critical for knot security

frame_analyses.description: 'C-loop' text overlay, third throw being constructed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Additional securing throw using C-loop technique

frame_analyses.description: Third throw construction continuing with good instrument coordination
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent technique maintained through multiple throws

frame_analyses.description: Final positioning of knot, suture ends being prepared for cutting
Final view shows completed knot on training pad, instruments removed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completed slip knot with securing throws visible

knot_assessments.notes: Slip knot technique used instead of surgeon's knot
Final securing throw using C-loop technique

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No drain visible in this training exercise, appears to be slip knot demonstration on training pad

technique_summary: Excellent demonstration of intracorporeal slip knot technique with proper C-loop formation and securing throws. The trainee showed good bimanual coordination and efficient knot construction with appropriate hand alternation between throws.
improvement_suggestions: Consider practicing traditional surgeon's knot technique in addition to slip knots
Work on faster transitions between throws to improve overall efficiency
strengths: Excellent slip knot technique with proper C-loop formation
Clean knot construction with good instrument control
